name: VNet_Flow_Logs
description: Azure VNet Flow Log entries forwarded via the vnet-flow-forwarder Azure Function to the LM REST Ingest API. Uses _lm.resourceId for resource mapping instead of Webhook Attribute extraction.

logFields.description: Source IP address of the flow
Destination IP address of the flow
Protocol name (TCP, UDP, ICMP, or IANA number)
Flow state: begin, continue, end, or deny
Bytes sent from source to destination
Bytes sent from destination to source
Packets sent from source to destination
Packets sent from destination to source
MAC address of the NIC (from blob path partition)
NSG or AVNM rule that evaluated the traffic
Log level: warn for DENY flows, info for all others
Static value: Azure VNet Flow Logs

resourceMapping.description: Maps to the Azure VNet resource by its full ARM resource ID. The Azure Function sets _lm.resourceId.azure.resourceid to the VNet's resource ID extracted from the flow log blob metadata.

azureFunctionConfig.deduplication: Block-level watermarks in Azure Table Storage
azureFunctionConfig.batchSize: Up to 7MB per LM API request (~14,000 flow entries)

steps: No LogSource configuration needed in the LM portal.
The REST Ingest API uses _lm.resourceId to map logs directly to devices.
Ensure the VNet (CTA-vnet) is discovered via Azure Cloud Monitoring integration.
Verify azure.resourceid property exists on the VNet device in LM.
Logs will appear in the Logs tab of the VNet device after the Function processes its first blob.
